input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    Title="MyMauiApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, shown as a top-level flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — single page flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants a .NET MAUI AppShell.xaml setup with a specific structure. This is a code generation/guidance request - I don't need to explore any codebase. I'll create the files directly.

Let me create:
1. `AppShell.xaml` with flyout structure
2. `AppShell.xaml.cs` with route registration

Structure:
- Flyout with:
  - Dashboard (single page)
  - Products (with tabs: Active, Archived)
  - Settings (single page)
- Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

data.content:
Here's the breakdown:

**`AppShell.xaml`** — Three `FlyoutItem` entries:

| Flyout Item | Structure |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | Single `ShellContent` → `DashboardPage` |
| **Products** | Two `<Tab>` children → `ActiveProductsPage` / `ArchivedProductsPage` (renders as bottom tabs) |
| **Settings** | Single `ShellContent` → `SettingsPage` |

**`AppShell.xaml.cs`** — Registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a programmatic route (not in the flyout). Navigate to it from a list like:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

Accept a query parameter on `ProductDetailsPage` with:

```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(ProductId), "id")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    public string ProductId { get; set; }
}
```

You'll need to create the five page classes under a `Pages/` folder (`DashboardPage`, `ActiveProductsPage`, `ArchivedProductsPage`, `SettingsPage`, `ProductDetailsPage`) and add matching icons to `Resources/Images`.
